content: EC-VERIFY-FIRST-001: re-read orchestrator-role/SKILL.md live off disk (not from Make's log) and confirmed zero '../../' substrings remain, six occurrences correctly resolved to full repo-relative paths, and no unrelated prose changed. Pass.

Requested item 1 wording check: true_intent's literal phrase would have routed all six remaining references to '<repo_path>/.agents/rules/', a location containing only the four Law files -- verified via Filesystem:list_directory that .agents/rules/ contains exactly 01-04 Law files, none named trail-schema/earned-constraints/hil-gating/ralph-loop-mechanics/new_trail_id. Literal compliance would have produced six broken references. Make's full-repo-relative-path alternative is flagged here as an interpretive substitution, not silently treated as equivalent to the literal instruction. Requires human confirmation this reading is correct.

Requested item 1, rename check: 'Usage (System Contract)' heading already present (from prior sealed trail 67f24fd5) -- re-confirmed live, not assumed from that trail's self-report. No 'Invocation Contract' string remains anywhere in the file. Pass.
Dependency-resolution-recorded (Rule 3): 00-deps.json present at this trail's root, resolved list matches planner/maker/checker/reflector actually dispatched. Pass.

Schema-conformance: brief-session.md frontmatter is valid YAML (description + Usage line) and matches the shape of sibling replay-trail.md/seal-trail.md exactly (description key, fenced parameter block, then prose). Pass.

Requested item 2 (Round Table simulation): brief-session.md's Round Table section enumerates the 10-domain roster from catalog/skills.json, sources each domain's status from that domain's own domain-scope/SKILL.md and .pmcro/trails/<domain>/ state, and explicitly forbids fabricating status for domains with no trails -- this satisfies 'simulate a Persona-Aware Round Table briefing for session hydration' without inventing ungrounded persona content. Pass.

Requested item 3 (Memory Bank convention): brief-session.md names an explicit convention (six Cline/Roo-Code-pattern files under .agents/memory-bank/), justified by an existing repo pattern (COLONY.md's .agents/ layering table) rather than an arbitrary choice, and ties progress.md's 'working' claims back to this Colony's own ACCEPT-disposition-trail verification rule rather than contradicting it. Pass. Note: the .agents/memory-bank/ directory and its six files do not yet exist on disk -- brief-session.md specifies them as create-on-first-run, and this cycle authored the command rather than executing it, so their absence is expected, not a defect.

Law-conformance: Law 4 (domain boundaries) -- this cycle's dispatching domain is cto, and both edited artifacts (orchestrator-role/SKILL.md, new brief-session.md) live under the Platform-tier orchestrator package, not under another C-Suite domain's own SKILL.md. cto's own domain-scope description explicitly covers 'PMCR-O loop/skill-pack design and validation', so this is in-scope for cto, not a Law 4 crossing into e.g. ceo's or coo's territory.

HIL gating: both artifacts live under catalog/. Per hil-gating.md this cycle cannot self-seal 'accept' on cleanliness alone. However: (a) neither write touched skills.json or a marketplace.json twin, renamed/removed an existing entry, or committed/pushed to git -- none of the enumerated TYPE1 triggers fired; (b) the human's dispatch named the exact file, the exact rename/path-style intent, and the exact new command file to create, matching the same explicit pre-authorization pattern documented in prior trail 67f24fd5's disposition.json. Flagging for orchestrator's disposition decision rather than deciding unilaterally which way this cuts.
